figures.caption: Table 2 Summary of the practical variants of the level-based algorithms, with their guarantees.
Table 1 Summary of low update time results for dynamic set cover in the high-frequency regime. Amortized results for update time and recourse are in black, worst-case results are in blue.
Figure 2 Performance profiles for amortized set cover size (left), amortized update time (center)

Figure 3 Performance profiles for each algorithm evaluated across all three quality metrics. Each row represents a specific algorithm (top to bottom: robust, local, partial, and global), divided into six variants with varying values of β. The columns represent amortized set cover size (left), update time (center), and recourse (right). Detailed legends for β values are provided beneath each row.

Figure 1 Normalized trade-off curves representing solution quality vs. efficiency. The left plot displays mean normalized set cover size vs. mean normalized update time, and the right plot displays mean normalized set cover size vs. mean normalized recourse. Each curve represents the geometric mean across 120 instances for the full range of β values; curves closer to the bottom-left corner indicate superior overall performance.
